library_preparation_preflight.summary: Lombok 1.18.20 has no transitive dependencies or service backend, but its meaningful use is as a javac annotation processor; a plain generated runtime test scaffold is unlikely to exercise it.
library_preparation_preflight.agent_guidance: Configure the generated tests to exercise Lombok through annotation processing, not only by importing annotation types. In Gradle terms this means adding the target Lombok artifact to the test annotation-processor path, e.g. `testAnnotationProcessor "org.projectlombok:lombok:$libraryVersion"`, or compiling small annotated source snippets from the test with `JavaCompiler` using an explicit `-processorpath` to the resolved Lombok jar. Official Lombok Gradle setup uses `compileOnly`/`annotationProcessor` and `testCompileOnly`/`testAnnotationProcessor`, not just a runtime dependency. Be aware that Lombok 1.18.20 only advertises JDK16 support; JDK21 support was added later in Lombok 1.18.30, so local Gradle verification may reject javac annotation-processing coverage for this exact version.
risks: Lombok 1.18.20 may fail as an annotation processor on the repository JDK21 toolchain because it predates Lombok's JDK21 support.
If tests only instantiate or inspect Lombok annotation classes, coverage will be scaffold-level and will not reflect normal library use.
Adding a newer Lombok version as a helper dependency would change the behavior under test and should be avoided.
